column1: # grep-tells.tsv — TIER 1 (flat-presence) rules for audit-swiftui-adaptive-layout (adl-01 … adl-07).
# severity: hard | warn | adv   (hard → runner exits 2). The runner greps each ERE over the target *.swift.
# This LOCATES only — the LLM agent READS each hit (SKILL.md step 3) and decides. This grep tier STANDS
# ALONE: ast-grep is NOT installed and NOT required. Structural absence calls (a NavigationSplitView with
# no horizontalSizeClass read anywhere in the file — adl-03/adl-04) are located broadly here and resolved by
# the agent in READ. Full ❌→✅ + sources: ../references/adaptive-layout-patterns.md (routed from SKILL.md).

column4: UIScreen.main / UIScreen.main.bounds used as a layout oracle — DEPRECATED on iOS 16+ (no scene/multitasking context). Read the container with GeometryReader or branch on size class; replace, never gate (adl-02). cross_ref uikit-overuse.

literal .frame(width: <number>) — full-screen content pinned to a device width letter-boxes on iPad / clips in landscape & Split View (adl-01). READ: does this wrap full-screen content or a genuinely fixed element?

NavigationSplitView shell — the regular-width split must adapt to compact width. READ the whole file: is there a horizontalSizeClass branch in scope? An unconditional split ships onto compact iPhone (adl-03). cross_ref adaptive-navigation.

size-class read present — confirm device-dependent arrangement actually BRANCHES on it; a layout file with arrangement logic and ZERO size-class reads never reacts to compact-vs-regular (adl-04). READ: is the class used to switch arrangement?
manual width-threshold ladder (size.width > …) switching fixed layouts — this is what ViewThatFits (iOS 16) expresses natively (adl-05). READ: are these mutually-exclusive fixed layouts?
a fractional-of-screen width by arithmetic (.width * 0.5 / .width / 2) — use containerRelativeFrame (iOS 17) for a fraction of the container (adl-06).

GeometryReader wrapped only to read a width threshold — if the decision is compact-vs-regular, use @Environment(\.horizontalSizeClass) instead of a proxy (adl-07). READ: is real per-pixel geometry needed, or just a size-class decision?
